title: [Bug]: Observability policy writer can emit non-standard JSON
summary: The observability policy writer uses default json.dumps behavior when writing raw dictionary policies, which can emit NaN or Infinity into policy files.
observed: A raw policy dictionary containing a non-finite value can be written to disk as non-standard JSON.
expected: The policy writer should use standards-compliant JSON emission and reject non-finite values before writing a file.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that attempts to write a raw observability policy dictionary containing NaN.
impact: Observability policies are security controls for metric sharing. They should remain portable, strict JSON documents and fail closed when invalid.
delivery_semantics: No JetStream ACK behavior changes. This fix affects optional observability policy generation only.
security: The fix must not print policy contents that may include local paths or operational naming details.
acceptance: A focused regression test proves raw policy dictionaries with non-finite values are rejected.
Valid ObservabilityPolicy instances continue to write atomically.
The error is reported as a configuration error.
Existing observability policy tests still pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, observability policy tests, observability CLI tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and observability documentation to mention strict JSON policy writing.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
